findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is always True in Python regardless of what `s` is or how Settings behaves. This test can never fail under any implementation.
findings.suggestion: Delete this test. If identity/singleton semantics need coverage, assert `get_settings() is get_settings()` (already covered by `test_get_settings_is_cached`). If the goal is a smoke-instantiation check, assert on a concrete default field value instead.

findings.message: Over-mocked: `mpatch.object` replaces the very property under test (`resolved_log_level`) with a lambda that returns 'MOCKED', then the assertion checks that the mock returns 'MOCKED'. This exercises only `unittest.mock` mechanics, never the real `resolved_log_level` implementation.
findings.suggestion: Delete this test. `resolved_log_level` is already covered by `test_resolved_log_level_is_debug_in_dev`, `test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev`, and `test_explicit_log_level_overrides_resolution`, which exercise the real method with real inputs. If a new branch needs coverage, add a concrete parametrize case to the existing parametrized test.
